explicit_text: I have a part-time salaried position as music director at a synagogue in Brookline, Massachusetts. This role involves choir direction, accompanying services, and managing the audio systems. Even during the pandemic, although the building is closed to the public, I prepare music for live streaming services from there. Brookline has been a steady base for part of my musical work for over twenty years.

This role involves choir direction, accompanying services, and managing the audio systems. Even during the pandemic, although the building is closed to the public, I prepare music for live streaming services from there. This town has been a steady base for part of my musical work for over twenty years.

explicit_text: Recently, I bought Final Cut Pro, which is Apple's high-end video editing software. I wanted to learn video editing to take some workload off a colleague who usually handles video for our synagogue services. Since I have extra free time now, I'm using this software to develop new skills that could become a future revenue stream. Video editing seems essential now, especially as music presentations increasingly require visuals.

explicit_text: Jay Cohen is a colleague at the synagogue who is very skilled with tech and video production. He usually assembles the video content for our live-streamed services, while I focus on the audio mixing. I've been sending him raw files to put together, but I want to learn more video editing myself to help him out since he's quite busy. Jay's expertise has been invaluable during the pandemic when we shifted to virtual services.

explicit_text: I live and work in the Boston area, which is rich with musical institutions and talented musicians. Boston's vibrant music scene includes many conservatory-trained players, and I often collaborate with them in ensembles and studios. The city's environment has shaped my career, even though my path was unconventional compared to many formally trained musicians here. Boston remains a hub for my musical activities and connections.

explicit_text: The New England Conservatory is one of the prestigious music schools in Boston that many musicians in the area attend. While I never went to music school myself, I often work alongside musicians who have trained there. It represents the formal side of music education in Boston, contrasting with my self-taught background. The presence of NEC contributes to the high level of musicianship in the local scene.

Although I didn't attend, I often encounter musicians from Boston Conservatory in ensembles and studio projects. Their presence highlights the strong academic music culture in Boston.

Berklee's influence is a big part of Boston's music scene.

explicit_text: Duke Ellington is an example I often think about when considering my own unconventional music career. Like me, he probably didn't attend formal music school but learned through playing and experience. He represents the tradition of great musicians who learned on the street and by doing rather than through academic training. While I don't compare myself to him, his path shows that there are many ways to succeed in music.

explicit_text: Charlie Parker is another legendary jazz musician who, like Duke Ellington, learned primarily through playing with others rather than formal education. I mention him as part of the lineage of musicians who developed their craft outside of conservatories. His example resonates with my own self-taught approach to music. It reminds me that practical experience and collaboration are powerful teachers in music.

explicit_text: YouTube has become an important platform for musicians to share their work and reach audiences, especially during the pandemic. Although I didn't mention specific projects on YouTube, the kind of video collaborations I do, like editing performances and creating multi-track videos, are often shared on platforms like YouTube. It represents the growing importance of video and online presence in a musician's career. Learning video editing with Final Cut Pro will help me create content suitable for YouTube and similar platforms.
